headline: AI Chip Competition Intensifies Amid Geopolitical Tensions; Macro Data Mixed
summary: Global markets are navigating heightened competition in the AI chip sector, with TSMC expanding US capex and Google exploring Samsung for future TPU production. Geopolitical concerns persist as China advances its domestic AI models and the US ITC investigates DRAM devices. Macro indicators show Korea's per capita GDP nearing $40,000, driven by semiconductor demand.

bullets.lead: TSMC is significantly increasing its US investment.

segments.text: The company plans to add $100 billion to its US spending plan, signaling continued commitment to advanced manufacturing capabilities outside Taiwan.

sources.title: TSMC Adds $100 Billion to Its U.S. Spending Plan

bullets.lead: Google is reportedly considering Samsung for future AI chip production.

segments.text: This move aims to diversify supply for its 10th-gen TPU, 'Icefish', as TSMC's capacity tightens due to Nvidia's AI chip demand and internal compute capacity constraints at Google.

bullets.lead: Broadcom and Powertech are forming a joint venture in Singapore.

segments.text: The $400 million investment will focus on producing high-end chip packaging substrates, including Powertech's Fan-Out Panel-Level Packaging.

bullets.lead: Memory is identified as a critical bottleneck in AI development.

segments.text: Jensen Huang highlighted this at CES 2026, leading to outperformance by Micron and Sandisk relative to Nvidia's stock since his remarks.

sources.title: Jensen Huang Told CES 2026 That Memory Is Now the Biggest Bottleneck in AI. Micron and Sandisk Have Outperformed Nvidia's Stock Ever Since.

bullets.lead: China's Moonshot AI is contributing to a chip selloff.

segments.text: The release of its Kimi K3 open-weight model has sparked debate on its commercial implications and potential to commoditize intelligence, pressuring frontier models.

sources.title: China’s Moonshot AI Adds More Fuel to Wall Street’s Chip Selloff
Meet Yang Zhilin, the CEO and founder behind China's buzzy new Kimi K3 AI model

bullets.lead: China is pushing a three-layer national AI strategy.

segments.text: This strategy focuses on domestic deployment, a sovereign tech stack, and international expansion, aiming for AI infrastructure self-reliance.

bullets.lead: PC brands are securing CXMT memory amid easing US-China tensions.

segments.text: Orders for ChangXin Memory Technology (CXMT) memory extend to end-2027, following reports of Apple testing CXMT memory.

bullets.lead: China is actively recruiting ASML engineers.

segments.text: This is part of a broader strategy involving '4 types of support' to bolster its domestic semiconductor industry.

sources.title: [人터뷰+] “中, ASML 엔지니어까지 빼간다...‘4종 지원책’으로 반도체...

bullets.lead: South Korea's per capita GDP is estimated to reach $39,000 this year.

segments.text: This projection, driven by the semiconductor boom, suggests the country is nearing the $40,000 mark for the first time.

sources.title: 올해 1인당 GDP 3만9000달러 추산…4만달러 첫 돌파 가능

bullets.lead: The market is experiencing a broad downturn.

segments.text: This is particularly notable in AI-related and Korean sectors, attributed to unprecedented leverage, margin calls, and forced liquidations.

bullets.lead: Fed communication is evolving with new leadership.

segments.text: Wall Street is adapting to a new era of communication, with discussions around how the Fed's new chair, Kevin Warsh, will approach inflation debates.

sources.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
Warsh Shows His Inner Hawk as Inflation Debate Heats Up

bullets.lead: US CPI has cooled while China's growth slows.

segments.text: These global economic trends are being closely monitored for their impact on monetary policy and market sentiment.

sources.title: US CPI Cools, China Growth Slows: The Global Economy in Charts

bullets.lead: The Trump administration plans to fund Maga-aligned projects in Europe.

segments.text: This initiative, reported by the Financial Times, signals a reordering of US aid and foreign policy priorities.

sources.title: Trump administration to fund Maga-aligned projects in Europe as he reorders US aid, FT reports
Trump to fund Maga-aligned projects in Europe as he reorders US aid

bullets.lead: Iran's supreme leader has accused Trump of violating a deal.

segments.text: This highlights ongoing tensions and potential for renewed conflict in the region, with the leader vowing a forceful response.

sources.title: Iran supreme leader accuses Trump of violating deal, vows forceful response

bullets.lead: Trump's Truth Social posts influence stock movements.

segments.text: Trump Media & Technology Group (DJT) reportedly pitched a $100,000 monthly fee for the fastest feed of the former president's posts, demonstrating the perceived value of his social media activity.

sources.title: See How Trump’s Truth Social Posts Move Stocks
Trump Media pitched $100,000 monthly fee for fastest feed of US president’s posts, sources say

bullets.lead: Big Law is preparing for a second confrontation with Trump.

segments.text: This relates to potential "capitulation deals" and legal challenges, indicating continued legal scrutiny surrounding the former president.

sources.title: Big Law braces for second fight with Trump over capitulation deals

bullets.lead: Meta Platforms is reportedly in talks to lease computing power to Anthropic.

segments.text: This potential deal, valued at up to $10 billion over two years, validates the "neocloud" business model where hyperscalers lease excess compute capacity.

sources.title: Meta擬出租AI算力給Anthropic 交易規模上看100億美元- 國際

bullets.lead: Oracle's data centers are facing multibillion-dollar cost surprises.

segments.text: Big Tech firms like Oracle are increasingly turning to bonds to finance their AI data center expansions, indicating significant capital expenditure in the sector.

sources.title: Exclusive: Oracle Data Centers Face Multibillion-Dollar Cost Surprises
Big Tech Firms Like Oracle Turn to Bonds to Finance A.I. Data Centers

bullets.lead: Google engineers are facing internal AI compute capacity constraints.

segments.text: Despite the company guiding $180-190 billion in capex this year, these constraints are reportedly leading to delays for Gemini 3.5 Pro.

bullets.lead: Nebius has secured $775 million in debt financing.

segments.text: This significantly oversubscribed debt, at attractive terms (SOFR + 2.50%), will be used for capacity expansion, demonstrating institutional confidence in its growth.

bullets.lead: The US ITC has initiated a Section 337 investigation into DRAM devices.

segments.text: This probe could impact the supply chain for certain dynamic random-access memory and downstream products, potentially affecting global memory markets.

bullets.lead: SK Hynix American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) are trading at a 25% premium.

segments.text: This 'reverse kimchi premium' is observed relative to the common stock, indicating strong investor demand for the ADRs.

sources.title: SK하이닉스 미국예탁주식, 본주 대비 25% 고평가 '역 김치 프리미엄' 현...

bullets.lead: Samsung and SK Hynix are under Washington's scrutiny.

segments.text: This relates to their memory fabs, indicating potential geopolitical and trade implications for the leading memory manufacturers.

sources.title: Commentary: Samsung, SK Hynix in Washington's crosshairs over memory fabs

watchlist.name: TSMC
watchlist.note: Watch for further details on US capex expansion and any impact on 2nm gross margin dilution concerns.

sources.title: TSMC Adds $100 Billion to Its U.S. Spending Plan

watchlist.note: Monitor AI compute capacity constraints and potential diversification of TPU manufacturing to Samsung.

watchlist.note: Keep an eye on the potential $10 billion deal to lease AI compute power to Anthropic, validating the "neocloud" model.

watchlist.name: Fed / Kevin Warsh
watchlist.note: Focus on upcoming communications regarding inflation and any shifts in monetary policy outlook from the new Fed leadership.

sources.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
Warsh Shows His Inner Hawk as Inflation Debate Heats Up

watchlist.note: Observe stock movements influenced by Trump's Truth Social posts and any developments regarding its premium feed.

x_themes.topic: Kimi K3 Open-Weight Model Release & Commercial Implications
x_themes.summary: Moonshot's Kimi K3, a large open-weight model, has been released, sparking debate on its commercial implications. While some see it commoditizing intelligence and pressuring frontier lab margins, others argue it drives demand for high-end silicon and hosting platforms, validating AI scaling laws despite its higher cost per token compared to previous Chinese LLMs.

x_themes.topic: Broad Market Downturn Driven by Leverage and Liquidations
x_themes.summary: The market is experiencing a significant downturn, particularly in AI-related and Korean sectors, driven by unprecedented levels of leverage, margin calls, and forced liquidations rather than fundamental issues, leading to a record TMT momentum unwind.

x_themes.topic: China's National AI Strategy and Infrastructure Investment Push
x_themes.summary: China's 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference outlined a 3-layer national AI strategy focusing on domestic deployment, a sovereign tech stack, and international expansion. This includes significant capital expenditure in AI infrastructure, embodied AI, industrial automation, and related sectors, with open-source models used as a geopolitical and distribution strategy.

x_themes.topic: Meta Leasing Compute Power to Anthropic
x_themes.summary: Meta is reportedly in talks to lease computing power to Anthropic in a deal potentially worth $10 billion over two years, validating the "neocloud" business model where hyperscalers provide compute to AI labs.

x_themes.topic: TSMC Q2 Earnings Beat Despite 2nm GM Dilution Concerns
x_themes.summary: TSMC reported a strong Q2 2026 earnings beat across revenue, gross margin, operating margin, net profit, and EPS, exceeding expectations despite concerns about 2nm gross margin dilution.

x_themes.topic: PC Brands Rush to Secure CXMT Memory Amid Easing US-China Tensions
x_themes.summary: PC brands are rushing to secure ChangXin Memory Technology (CXMT) memory, with orders extending to end-2027, following reports of eased US-China tensions and Apple testing CXMT memory and lobbying the US government, signaling a potential green light for its use.

x_themes.topic: Goldman Sachs Raises Innolight PT on Strong Optical Sector Outlook
x_themes.summary: Goldman Sachs significantly raised Innolight's price target and 2026-2028 earnings estimates, citing much higher silicon photonics module volumes, 1.6T/3.2T lifting ASPs/margins, and increased AI capex, signaling strong demand for the broader optical sector.

x_themes.topic: US Air Force Triples National Security Space Launch Contract Ceiling
x_themes.summary: The US Air Force has more than tripled the ceiling for its National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contracts to $17 billion from $5.6 billion, increasing opportunities for eligible providers like SpaceX and Rocket Lab.

x_themes.topic: SpaceX in Talks to Provide Computing Power for Pentagon
x_themes.summary: SpaceX is reportedly in discussions with the Pentagon to provide computing capacity, with a potential deal valued at up to several billion dollars.

x_themes.topic: Taiwan Increases Science & Tech Spending for Key Industries
x_themes.summary: Taiwan's National Science and Technology Council plans to increase spending on science and technology projects by 6.2% next year, targeting key areas including semiconductors, chip innovation, next-generation telecommunications, quantum computing, robotics, space programs (LEO satellites), sovereign AI compute, and smart government systems.

x_themes.topic: Google's Internal AI Compute Capacity Constraints and Gemini Delays
x_themes.summary: Google engineers are reportedly facing internal AI compute capacity constraints, despite the company guiding $180-190 billion in capex this year, leading to delays for Gemini 3.5 Pro and insufficient compute for engineers required to use AI for coding.

x_themes.topic: Broadcom and Powertech Form JV for Advanced Packaging in Singapore
x_themes.summary: Broadcom and Taiwan's Powertech are investing $400 million in a joint venture plant in Singapore to produce high-end chip packaging substrates, including Powertech's Fan-Out Panel-Level-Packaging (FOPLP) technology for large AI data center chips.

x_themes.topic: Nebius Secures $775M Debt for Capacity Expansion
x_themes.summary: Nebius ($NBIS) successfully raised $775 million in debt financing at attractive terms (SOFR + 2.50%), which was significantly oversubscribed, demonstrating institutional confidence in its assets and execution as it expands capacity for clients like Microsoft.

x_themes.topic: US ITC Institutes Section 337 Investigation into DRAM Devices
x_themes.summary: The U.S. International Trade Commission has initiated a Section 337 investigation into certain dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) devices and downstream products, which could impact Samsung and the broader DRAM industry.
